releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

### Folder Rename Duplicates (#74)
Fixed an issue where renaming or moving folders would create duplicate files on OneDrive instead of properly renaming them.

**What was happening:**
- When you renamed a folder locally, the plugin treated it as a new folder creation
- The old folder and its contents remained on OneDrive
- This resulted in duplicate files at both the old and new locations

**What's fixed:**
- Folder renames now use OneDrive's atomic MOVE API instead of delete+create
- All child files and subfolders are properly updated to reflect the new path
- No more duplicate files when renaming or moving folders

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.4.3...1.4.4

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Code Quality & Test Coverage
- **Removed unused dependency**: Removed \@azure/msal-node\ - the plugin uses a custom OAuth implementation for cross-platform compatibility (iOS/Android)
- **Coverage thresholds enforced**: CI now fails if coverage drops below 80% for critical sync/API code
- **Expanded test suite**: 354 → 389 tests (+10%)
- **Overall coverage**: 72% → 75%
- **Critical file coverage**:
  - \oneDriveClient.ts\: 68% → 93%
  - \chunkUpload.ts\: 81% → 100%
  - \conflictResolver.ts\: 72% → 100%

### Internal Improvements
- Refactored SyncEngine constructor for better maintainability
- Added module documentation headers across codebase
- Removed dead code in delta pagination logic
- Fixed ESLint warnings with proper typing

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.4.2...1.4.3

## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- **Fixed App Folder subpath path mapping** - Files now sync to the correct paths when using App Folder mode with a vault subfolder (#66)
- **Fixed concurrent sync operations** - Reconcile from cloud and regular sync now share a mutex to prevent simultaneous runs (#66)
- **Removed duplicate connect/disconnect notices** (#64)

### New Features
- **Experimental settings** - New performance tuning options: skip folder checks, max concurrent operations, atomic moves (#60, #61, #62)
- **Enhanced rename/move logging** - Better diagnostics for duplicate file issues (#59)

### Security
- **Upgraded @azure/msal-node to v5.2.5** - Removes uuid dependency with known vulnerability (#65)

### Maintenance

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.3.8...1.4.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🐛 Critical Bug Fix: Data Loss During Auto-Sync

This release fixes a critical bug where typing while auto-sync was running could cause your edits to be overwritten (#49).

### What was happening
When you edited a file and sync uploaded it, then continued typing before the next sync, the plugin would incorrectly download the older version from OneDrive — losing your latest edits.

### Root cause
The plugin was comparing local filesystem timestamps against OneDrive server timestamps to decide which version was "newer". Since server timestamps are assigned when uploads complete (not when content was created), this comparison was unreliable. The fix uses **content hashes** instead — if the remote "change" matches what we uploaded, we recognize it's not a real conflict and upload local edits instead.

### New setting
- **Sync on file change** toggle — disable to prevent auto-sync while actively editing (manual/periodic sync still work)

---

## 🌍 Internationalization Support

Thanks to **@jiaoyingxing** for contributing i18n support! The plugin UI now supports multiple languages:

- 🇺🇸 English (default)
- 🇨🇳 Simplified Chinese (zh-CN)

The language is automatically detected from your Obsidian settings. More languages can be added by contributing translation files.

---

## 🧹 Housekeeping

- Removed unused modal classes (dead code cleanup)
- Improved issue triage workflow to require actual debug logs

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.3.7...1.3.8

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🐛 Bug Fix: Data Loss During Auto-Sync

This beta fixes a critical bug where typing while auto-sync was running could cause your edits to be overwritten (#49).

### What was happening
When you edited a file and sync uploaded it, then continued typing before the next sync, the plugin would incorrectly download the older version from OneDrive — losing your latest edits.

### Root cause
The plugin was comparing local filesystem timestamps against OneDrive server timestamps to decide which version was "newer". These clocks are different and the comparison was meaningless — the server timestamp was always later because it's assigned when the upload completes.

### The fix
Instead of comparing timestamps, the plugin now compares **content hashes**. When the delta API reports a "change" that matches what we already uploaded (same hash), we recognize it's just our own upload echoing back — not a real conflict. Local edits are now correctly uploaded.

### New setting
- **Sync on file change** toggle — disable to prevent auto-sync while actively editing (manual/periodic sync still work)

---

⚠️ **This is a beta release for testing via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat).** Please report any issues!
releases.name: Beta 1.3.8-beta.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.3.7...1.3.8-beta.0
releases.name: Beta 1.3.8-beta.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🔒 Security: Token Storage Migration

Authentication tokens (OAuth access & refresh tokens) are now stored using Obsidian's **SecretStorage API** instead of `data.json`. This is a significant security improvement — tokens are no longer sitting in a plain-text JSON file in your vault.

### What's new

- **Secure token storage** — tokens are stored via Obsidian's platform-native SecretStorage (available since Obsidian 1.11.4) rather than base64-obfuscated values in `data.json`
- **Automatic migration** — existing users' tokens migrate seamlessly on first load; no action needed
- **data.json cleaned** — after migration, token fields are removed from `data.json` so they're no longer exposed on disk
- **Status bar spacing** — fixed default spacing between sync icon and status text

### Why this matters

Previously, anyone with access to your vault files (cloud backup, shared drive, disk access) could trivially decode your OneDrive auth tokens from `data.json`. The plugin folder was excluded from sync to prevent this, but the tokens were still on disk. Now they're in Obsidian's secure storage, which uses platform-appropriate mechanisms.

### Upgrade notes

- **Minimum Obsidian version**: 1.12.0 (unchanged)
- Migration is automatic — just update the plugin and reload
- The `.obsidian/plugins/onedrive-sync/` folder exclusion remains in place to prevent self-downgrade and sync state conflicts

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Fix chunked upload on desktop**: Remove forbidden `Content-Length` header that caused `ERR_INVALID_ARGUMENT` in Chromium/Electron — the HTTP stack sets it automatically from the body
- **Fix chunked upload completion**: Capture the completed item directly from the final chunk response (200/201) instead of a separate GET request that returns 404 after the upload session is cleaned up
- **Fix delta resync detection**: Make resync/token-expired detection case-insensitive to correctly handle `Resync required` responses from Microsoft Graph
- **Small file upload**: Use Obsidian's `requestUrl` instead of Graph SDK's `Buffer.from()` for cross-platform compatibility (Android/iOS)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New

### Bug Fixes
- **Android/iOS upload fix**: Replaced Graph SDK .put() in uploadSmallFile with Obsidian's cross-platform requestUrl fetch wrapper. The Graph SDK internally calls Buffer.from() which is a Node.js built-in unavailable on Android/iOS, causing uploads to fail on mobile.
- **Improved error logging**: logger.ts now properly serializes Error objects (extracting name, message, and stack) so errors are visible in vault log output instead of showing as {}.
- **Retry logging**: Error details are now surfaced at the point where retry attempts stop, making it easier to diagnose sync failures.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.3.3...1.3.4

releases.name: Beta 1.3.4-beta.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Fixed small file upload timeouts (504)**: Replaced \putStream()\ with \put()\ for files under 4MB. \putStream()\ expects a Node.js stream, not an \ArrayBuffer\, causing the server to hang until timeout.
- **Improved retry handling**: Transient errors (408, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504) from the Microsoft Graph SDK are now correctly recognized as retryable, even when thrown as raw SDK errors rather than typed \OneDriveError\ instances.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Config file deletes from cloud now sync locally** — Cloud-side deletions of `.obsidian/` config files (e.g. removing a plugin from OneDrive) were silently ignored because the vault API can't see config paths. Now checks tracked state to properly delete config files via the adapter.

- **Config folder cleanup after cloud delete** — Empty plugin folders (e.g. `.obsidian/plugins/calendar/`) were left behind after their files were deleted from cloud. Now falls back to adapter for config folder removal.

- **404 on delete no longer aborts sync** — When deleting a remote item that's already gone (e.g. after a partial sync failure or 504 outage), the 404 error was treated as fatal and aborted the entire sync, preventing remaining operations from completing. Now treated as success since the item is already removed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 🗂️ Folder Sync Support

### New Features
- **Empty folder creates** now sync to OneDrive — creating an empty folder in your vault pushes it to the cloud
- **Empty folder deletes** now sync to OneDrive — deleting an empty folder locally removes it from the cloud
- **Cloud-deleted empty folders** are now removed locally when the delta API reports the deletion

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed `markInitialSyncDone()` not being called on zero-operation syncs, which blocked folder event detection
- Fixed early return in sync flow that skipped `deleteCloudDeletedFolders` when no file operations were planned

### Improvements
- Replaced blanket `pruneAllEmptyFolders` with explicit folder event tracking — empty folders the user intentionally created are no longer auto-deleted
- Narrowed remote folder pruning to config folders only (`.obsidian/plugins/*`) since regular folders now use explicit `FOLDER_DELETE` events
- Added 7 new EventManager tests for TFolder create/delete/rename events (300 total tests)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New in 1.3.0

### Sync Reliability
- **Plugin folder deletion now propagates correctly** — deleting a plugin locally removes its files and folder from OneDrive
- **Chunked upload fix** — files >4MB (e.g. Excalidraw) no longer fail with `ERR_INVALID_ARGUMENT`. Chunk sizes are now aligned to 320 KiB multiples per Microsoft Graph spec
- **ENOENT resilience** — files that vanish mid-sync no longer crash the entire sync batch; they're converted to remote deletes
- **Config file deletion detection** — `.obsidian/` file deletions are now caught via raw events instead of being silently ignored
- **Cloud-deleted folder safety** — incoming folder deletes only remove local folders if they're empty after file deletions (preserves untracked files like data.json)
- **Reconcile ghost cleanup** — tracked state entries for previously-deleted files are now properly purged during reconcile
- **No more false re-uploads** — undefined content hashes after reconcile are silently backfilled instead of triggering unnecessary uploads
- **Settings toggles no longer reset sync** — toggling app/plugin sync settings no longer wipes delta cursors and tracked state

### Logging Improvements
- **Log level setting** — new dropdown (Off / Error / Warn / Info / Debug) replaces the old debug toggle. Default is Off
- **Shorter log format** — `[2026-06-07T01:35:39Z] [DBG] message` instead of verbose plugin name and level
- **Plugin version logged** at the start of each sync for easier troubleshooting

### UI
- **Sync Now button** moved into the Sync Status row for a cleaner layout
- **Removed toast notifications** when toggling sync settings

### Testing
- **293 tests passing** with comprehensive deletion scenario coverage
- 16 new deletion permutation tests (local→remote, remote→local, folder cleanup, reconcile)
- Full test documentation in `tests/SYNC-TESTS.md`

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.2.2...1.3.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.2.1 — Config File Sync & UX Improvements

### ✨ New Features

- **`.obsidian/` config file sync** — Plugin manifests, app settings, appearance, hotkeys, and community plugin lists now sync bidirectionally with OneDrive when enabled in settings. Changes are detected instantly via Obsidian's file system events.

- **Progress bar notifications** — Sync operations with 5+ files now show a real progress bar (using Obsidian's built-in `ProgressBarComponent`) instead of text-only notices.

### 🛠 Improvements

- **Smarter change detection** — Config files are compared by content hash (FNV-1a), not just modification time. This prevents unnecessary uploads when Obsidian touches config files on startup without changing their content.

- **Cleaner notifications** — Removed redundant "X files synced" and "Everything up to date" popup notices. The status bar already provides this information. Only conflict and error notifications remain.

- **Popout window compatibility** — Timer APIs now use `window.*` methods for compatibility with Obsidian's popout windows. Progress bar falls back gracefully when DOM is unavailable.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fixed config files never uploading despite sync settings being enabled (adapter API fallback for non-TFile paths)
- Fixed config files re-syncing on every plugin reload (startup event suppression + content hash comparison)
- Fixed flaky `logManager` test caused by insufficient microtask flushes
- Removed settings page title heading that caused display issues

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.1.1...1.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## v1.2.0 — Config File Sync & UX Improvements

### ✨ New Features

- **`.obsidian/` config file sync** — Plugin manifests, app settings, appearance, hotkeys, and community plugin lists now sync bidirectionally with OneDrive when enabled in settings. Changes are detected instantly via Obsidian's file system events.

- **Progress bar notifications** — Sync operations with 5+ files now show a real progress bar (using Obsidian's built-in `ProgressBarComponent`) instead of text-only notices.

### 🛠 Improvements

- **Smarter change detection** — Config files are compared by content hash (FNV-1a), not just modification time. This prevents unnecessary uploads when Obsidian touches config files on startup without changing their content.

- **Cleaner notifications** — Removed redundant "X files synced" and "Everything up to date" popup notices. The status bar already provides this information. Only conflict and error notifications remain.

- **Settings page header** — Now shows "OneDrive Sync" as a proper heading with version number and a clickable GitHub link.

- **Popout window compatibility** — Timer APIs now use `window.*` methods for compatibility with Obsidian's popout windows. Progress bar falls back gracefully when DOM is unavailable.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fixed config files never uploading despite sync settings being enabled (adapter API fallback for non-TFile paths)
- Fixed config files re-syncing on every plugin reload (startup event suppression + content hash comparison)
- Fixed flaky `logManager` test caused by insufficient microtask flushes

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/JeffSteinbok/obsidian-onedrive/compare/1.1.1...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## ⚠️ Breaking Change

This release includes a **new Azure app registration**. Existing users will need to **disconnect and re-link to OneDrive** in the plugin settings after updating.

Your files in OneDrive are not affected — the plugin will re-sync on first connection.

## What's Changed

### App Folder Mode Fixes
- **New app registration** with a clean, consistent folder name (`ObsidianOneDrive`)
- **Dynamic app folder discovery** — the plugin now detects the actual app folder path at runtime instead of relying on a hardcoded constant
- **Fixed first-sync errors** — `.obsidian` subfolder 404 errors are now handled gracefully when the app folder is brand new
- **Fixed upload path construction** — uploads now go directly to the app folder root instead of trying to create `/Apps` from the drive root (which fails with App Folder permissions)

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed `isItemNotFoundError` to catch Graph SDK errors (`GraphError` has `statusCode`/`code` properties but is not an `OneDriveError` instance)
- Improved shared folder support for vaults nested inside shared folders (from 1.0.5)

### UI
- Settings no longer show a hardcoded app folder path — descriptions are now generic since the actual folder name is determined by Azure

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.5

### Bug Fixes

- **Fix shared folder sync when vault is nested inside shared root** — When using a shared OneDrive folder where the vault was in a subfolder (e.g. `SharedFolder/Vaults/MyVault`), sync would fail with a 404 error. Graph API paths were incorrectly constructed relative to the shared root instead of the vault folder. Both delta queries and file operations are now correctly scoped.

### Improvements

- **Refactored sync engine** — Broke up the large `performSync()` method into focused, well-documented helper methods for better readability and maintainability.
- **Centralized timer API** — Extracted shared `timerApi` utility to eliminate `globalThis` references flagged by the Obsidian review bot.
- **Removed all eslint-disable directives** — Cleaner code that passes the Obsidian community plugin review without directive comment errors.
- **Added artifact attestations** — Release assets (`main.js`, `styles.css`) now include cryptographic build provenance via GitHub artifact attestations.
- **Updated README** — Installation instructions now reference the Obsidian Community Plugin Store.
- **Added BRAT pre-release workflow** — Beta testers can now get pre-release builds via BRAT using `manifest-beta.json`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# OneDrive Sync for Obsidian — Initial Release

Sync your Obsidian vault with OneDrive Personal/Consumer. Works on mobile and desktop.

## Features

- **Two-way sync** — local changes upload to OneDrive, remote changes download automatically
- **Delta sync** — uses the OneDrive delta API for fast, incremental syncs (no full scans)
- **Mobile support** — designed for Obsidian Mobile on iOS and Android
- **Conflict resolution** — visual diff viewer for side-by-side conflict resolution
- **Folder-scoped sync** — choose any OneDrive folder as your sync root
- **Selective sync** — `.syncignore` file support for excluding paths
- **Large delete safety** — configurable threshold warns before bulk deletes
- **Plugin & settings sync** — optionally sync community plugin manifests and app settings across devices
- **Device code authentication** — secure OAuth2 device code flow (no passwords stored)
- **Automatic periodic sync** — configurable sync interval with debounced event-driven sync

## Getting Started

1. Install the plugin from the Obsidian Community Plugin directory
2. Open Settings → OneDrive Sync
3. Sign in with your Microsoft account using the device code flow
4. Select a OneDrive folder to sync with
5. Tap "Sync Now" or enable automatic sync

## Requirements

- Obsidian 1.12.0 or later
- A Microsoft account with OneDrive Personal/Consumer storage
